title: Restrict mode on mounted Fernet key Secret volumes
description:
# GitHub Issue #300: Restrict mode on mounted Fernet/credential key Secret volumes to silence Keystone's world-readable key_repository warning

**Category**: bug | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Every Keystone pod reconciled by the operator emits the following warning on startup:

```
2026-04-28 14:32:43.466 12 WARNING keystone.common.fernet_utils [None req-f046276d-3ae1-43e5-9729-054a91337e86 - - - - - -] key_repository is world readable: /etc/keystone/fernet-keys
```

The check originates upstream in `keystone.common.fernet_utils.FernetUtils._check_key_repository` and fires whenever the directory hosting the Fernet repository carries the world-read bit (`stat.S_IROTH`). The exact same `FernetUtils` class is reused by the credential provider against `/etc/keystone/credential-keys` (`keystone.credential.providers.fernet.Provider`), so the credential repository will emit the analogous warning under identical conditions — the fernet line just lands first in the log on cold start. The defect is symmetric and both repositories must be fixed together.

Root cause is the volume layout in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:246-261`. Both `SecretVolumeSource` blocks are constructed without a `DefaultMode`, so Kubernetes falls back to the volume default — `0644` for projected files and `0755` for the projected directory, both world-readable. Combined with the absence of `PodSecurityContext.FSGroup` on the pod (`reconcile_deployment.go:166-167` sets only `TerminationGracePeriodSeconds`, `TopologySpreadConstraints`, `PriorityClassName`; the container-level `restrictedSecurityContext()` at `security_context.go:21-35` neither does nor can set `fsGroup`), kubelet leaves both projected directories at `0755`.

The same construction is reused by the rotation CronJob templates:

- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:357-378` — `fernet-keys-src` Secret volume with no `DefaultMode`; the init container `cp /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/` (`reconcile_fernet.go:314`) targets an `EmptyDir` working copy that inherits a world-readable mode.
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go` — symmetric credential rotation CronJob with the same defect.

Operationally this is a noise + defence-in-depth issue. The keystone process runs as UID/GID `42424` (`security_context.go:16,25-26`) and `ReadOnlyRootFilesystem=true` plus the PSS Restricted profile (CC-0045) bound the in-cluster blast radius. But:

- The warning trains operators to ignore `WARNING` lines from `keystone.common.fernet_utils`, masking real issues at the same severity (e.g. owner-mismatch warnings emitted by the same function).
- A future log-based alert or an upstream promotion of the check to `ERROR` would page on every pod start across the fleet.
- Mounted Fernet keys are active token-signing material and credential keys protect every persisted EC2/application credential at rest in the Keystone DB; there is no reason any sidecar, ephemeral debug container, or future init container co-scheduled into the pod's mount namespace should be able to read them.

**Motivation**: Reproduced on a single-replica Keystone pod against current `main`. The warning is deterministic, fires on every cold start before `--master` worker fork, and is currently unblocked only by ignoring it. The fix is mechanical:

1. Set `PodSecurityContext.FSGroup = openstackUID` (42424) on the Keystone pod and on both rotation CronJob pods. With FSGroup set, kubelet projects the Secret volume directory at mode `0750` (owner `root`, group `42424`, group-readable but not world-readable) and applies group-read on files via the standard `defaultMode | 050` projection — the openstack process can still read its own keys via the group bit.
2. Add `DefaultMode: ptr.To(int32(0o400))` on every `fernet-keys` / `credential-keys` `SecretVolumeSource` (effective file mode becomes `0450` after the FSGroup `| 050` projection — owner-read + group-read, no world bits).
3. Update the rotation init container `cp` invocation to `install -m 0400 /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/` (or `cp -p` followed by `chmod 0400`) so the EmptyDir working copy is non-world-readable after the rotation pod hands off to keystone-manage.

Both Fernet and credential paths share identical builder shape (`reconcile_fernet.go` ↔ `reconcile_credential.go`), so one symmetric fix covers both.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` (`buildKeystoneDeployment`, PodSpec at L166-167, Secret volumes at L246-261)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go` (`fernetRotateCronJob` Pod template L357-378, init container at L314)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go` (symmetric credential rotation CronJob)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go` (currently asserts volume/mount existence at L280-291, L303-315 but no mode assertions)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet_test.go`, `reconcile_credential_test.go` (rotation CronJob mode assertions)
- `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md` (volume table currently lists `defaultMode: 0555` for the rotation scripts ConfigMap but no mode for either key Secret volume)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `buildKeystoneDeployment` sets `Spec.Template.Spec.SecurityContext.FSGroup = ptr.To(openstackUID)` (reusing the constant from `security_context.go:16`) and `DefaultMode = ptr.To(int32(0o400))` on both the `fernet-keys` and `credential-keys` `SecretVolumeSource`.
- [ ] Fernet and credential rotation CronJob templates apply the same `FSGroup` on the Pod and the same `DefaultMode` on every `*-keys-src` Secret volume.
- [ ] The rotation init container preserves a non-world-readable mode on the EmptyDir working copy (`install -m 0400 ...` or `cp -p` + `chmod 0400`), so post-rotation the keystone container still mounts `/etc/keystone/fernet-keys` non-world-readable.
- [ ] `reconcile_deployment_test.go` asserts `Spec.Template.Spec.SecurityContext.FSGroup == 42424` and `DefaultMode == 0o400` on both Secret volumes in the rendered Deployment.
- [ ] `reconcile_fernet_test.go` and `reconcile_credential_test.go` assert the same on the rotation CronJob and assert the init-container command argv contains the non-world-readable copy/chmod step.
- [ ] An integration or chainsaw test scrapes the keystone container log for 30 s after pod-Ready and asserts the `key_repository is world readable` substring is absent for *both* `fernet-keys` and `credential-keys` paths (negative assertion for each, since the fernet line currently masks the credential line).
- [ ] No regression in `chainsaw/keystone-fernet-rotation` or `chainsaw/keystone-credential-rotation` — the keystone-manage rotation step still reads/writes the working copy under the new permissions.
- [ ] `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md` documents the chosen mode for both Secret volumes alongside the existing `0555` note for the rotation script ConfigMap, with a one-line rationale referencing Keystone's `_check_key_repository` check.

**Refs**: CC-0045 (PSS Restricted profile / `restrictedSecurityContext`), upstream `keystone/common/fernet_utils.py::_check_key_repository`.

**Labels:** bug

**Labels:** bug

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/300

stories.title: Operator silences the world-readable key_repository warning on Keystone pod startup (CC-0099)
stories.want: the keystone container logs to be free of the recurring `key_repository is world readable` WARNING for both /etc/keystone/fernet-keys and /etc/keystone/credential-keys
stories.so_that: real WARNING-level signals from keystone.common.fernet_utils stay actionable and a future log-based alert does not page on every pod start
criteria: After a fresh `kubectl rollout` of a Keystone pod, scraping the keystone container log for 30 s post-Ready returns zero matches for the substring `key_repository is world readable`

The negative log assertion holds for both `/etc/keystone/fernet-keys` and `/etc/keystone/credential-keys` paths (both must be checked because the fernet warning currently masks the credential one on cold start)
Other keystone WARNING lines (owner-mismatch from the same `_check_key_repository`, oslo.config deprecations) remain visible — the fix is targeted at the `world readable` substring only
The log scrape runs as part of the Keystone CI chainsaw suite and fails the test on regression

stories.title: Cluster security reviewer can verify Fernet/credential key files are not world-readable on the rendered Pod spec (CC-0099)
stories.want: the rendered Keystone Deployment and rotation CronJob PodSpecs to set a FSGroup of 42424 and DefaultMode 0o400 on every Secret volume that carries Fernet or credential keys
stories.so_that: I can audit the cluster for defence-in-depth on token-signing and credential-encryption material without reaching into a live pod's mount namespace
criteria: `kubectl get deploy <name> -o yaml` on the operator-managed Deployment shows `spec.template.spec.securityContext.fsGroup: 42424`
Both `fernet-keys` and `credential-keys` `secret` volume entries in the rendered Deployment carry `defaultMode: 256` (0o400)
Both rotation CronJobs (`{name}-fernet-rotate`, `{name}-credential-rotate`) carry the same `fsGroup: 42424` and the `*-keys-src` Secret volumes carry `defaultMode: 256` (0o400)
Unit tests in the controller package assert these values numerically — no implicit defaults
The reviewer does not need to inspect a running pod: every guarantee is visible on the rendered spec

stories.title: Operator preserves Fernet/credential key rotation correctness under the stricter file mode (CC-0099)
stories.want: the fernet-rotate and credential-rotate CronJobs to keep producing valid rotated keys when the working-copy EmptyDir is non-world-readable
stories.so_that: the security hardening does not break the existing rotation path or keystone-manage's ability to read/write the working copy
criteria: The init container `copy-keys` copies the source Secret onto the EmptyDir working copy with mode 0o400 owned by openstack:openstack (UID 42424)
The keystone-manage `fernet_rotate` / `credential_rotate` step running in the rotation container can still read the working copy and write the new primary key

After rotation, the keystone container — which mounts the production Secret read-only with `defaultMode: 0o400` and `fsGroup: 42424` — picks up the new key without rollout (`pod-template-hash`-labelled pod UIDs unchanged) and validates a pre-rotation token (existing fernet-rotation chainsaw guarantees still hold)
Equivalent assertions hold for credential-rotate (existing credential-rotation chainsaw still passes)

stories.title: Future maintainer reads in the reconciler reference docs which mode applies to which key Secret volume and why (CC-0099)
stories.want: the keystone-reconciler reference doc to document the chosen `fsGroup` and `defaultMode` values for both Fernet and credential key volumes alongside the existing `0555` note for the rotation script ConfigMap
stories.so_that: I do not regress these values in a future refactor and I understand the upstream Keystone check (`_check_key_repository`) that motivates them
criteria: `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md` Volume tables for the API Deployment and for both rotation CronJobs list `defaultMode: 0400` for every key Secret volume and `fsGroup: 42424` on the PodSpec
Each row carries a one-line rationale referencing Keystone's `_check_key_repository` check and CC-0099

stories.title: Developer onboarding to the rotation path can read the test asserting the install/chmod step in the init container argv (CC-0099)
stories.role: developer onboarding to the rotation path
stories.want: the existing rotation CronJob unit tests to spell out the exact init-container shell argv that preserves the non-world-readable mode on the EmptyDir working copy
stories.so_that: a future change to `cp` semantics or a switch to a different copy mechanism cannot silently regress the file mode
criteria: `reconcile_fernet_test.go` asserts the init-container `Command` argv contains an `install -m 0400` (or equivalent `cp` + `chmod 0400`) invocation
`reconcile_credential_test.go` asserts the same for the credential rotate init container
The test failure message names CC-0099 so a future developer hitting the assertion finds this feature plan

requirements.description: buildKeystoneDeployment SHALL set Spec.Template.Spec.SecurityContext.FSGroup to ptr.To(openstackUID) (42424) so kubelet projects every Secret volume directory at mode 0750 instead of 0755 (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: FSGroup chgrp's the projected Secret directory onto the openstack group; without it kubelet leaves the directory at 0755 which carries S_IROTH and triggers Keystone's `_check_key_repository` warning

scenarios.name: FSGroup is set on the rendered Deployment
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is invoked with any valid Keystone CR
scenarios.then: the returned Deployment has Spec.Template.Spec.SecurityContext != nil and *Spec.Template.Spec.SecurityContext.FSGroup == 42424
and_then: the value reuses the openstackUID constant from security_context.go (no duplicated literal in reconcile_deployment.go)

scenarios.name: FSGroup is the only PodSecurityContext field set
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is invoked
scenarios.then: Spec.Template.Spec.SecurityContext.RunAsUser, RunAsGroup, RunAsNonRoot, SeccompProfile, FSGroupChangePolicy remain nil
and_then: container-level SecurityContext (returned by restrictedSecurityContext()) is unchanged — pod- and container-level remain orthogonal so PSS Restricted enforcement at the container is not duplicated at the pod

scenarios.name: FSGroup does not regress when the CR sets unrelated fields
scenarios.when: the Keystone CR sets PriorityClassName, TopologySpreadConstraints, or a non-default replica count
scenarios.then: FSGroup remains 42424 in the rendered PodSpec

requirements.description: buildKeystoneDeployment SHALL set DefaultMode to ptr.To(int32(0o400)) on both the `fernet-keys` and `credential-keys` SecretVolumeSource entries (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: With FSGroup set, kubelet effective file mode becomes `defaultMode | 050`; setting `defaultMode = 0400` yields `0450` (owner-read + group-read, no world bits) — sufficient for the openstack process which runs in group 42424

scenarios.name: fernet-keys volume carries defaultMode 0o400
scenarios.when: the rendered Deployment Volumes are inspected
scenarios.then: the Volume named `fernet-keys` has Secret != nil and *Secret.DefaultMode == int32(0o400) (256 decimal)
and_then: no other Volume mode is changed by this requirement

scenarios.name: credential-keys volume carries defaultMode 0o400
scenarios.when: the rendered Deployment Volumes are inspected
scenarios.then: the Volume named `credential-keys` has Secret != nil and *Secret.DefaultMode == int32(0o400)

scenarios.name: config ConfigMap volume is left untouched
scenarios.when: the rendered Deployment Volumes are inspected
scenarios.then: the Volume named `config` has ConfigMap.DefaultMode == nil (unchanged)
and_then: the test guards against accidental scope creep onto unrelated volumes

requirements.description: fernetRotationCronJob SHALL set FSGroup=42424 on the Pod template and DefaultMode=0o400 on the `fernet-keys-src` Secret volume; the read-only `credential-keys` Secret volume mounted by the fernet rotate Pod SHALL also carry DefaultMode=0o400 (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: The rotation Pod mounts the production fernet Secret via `fernet-keys-src` and the production credential Secret read-only via `credential-keys`; both must follow the same hardening as the API Deployment so a co-scheduled debug/sidecar container in the rotation Pod cannot world-read either Secret

scenarios.name: fernet rotation Pod has FSGroup 42424
scenarios.when: fernetRotationCronJob is rendered
scenarios.then: the JobTemplate Pod template Spec.SecurityContext.FSGroup == 42424

scenarios.name: fernet-keys-src carries defaultMode 0o400
scenarios.when: the rendered CronJob Volumes are inspected
scenarios.then: the Volume named `fernet-keys-src` has Secret != nil and *Secret.DefaultMode == int32(0o400)

scenarios.name: credential-keys (read-only mount) carries defaultMode 0o400
scenarios.when: the rendered CronJob Volumes are inspected
scenarios.then: the Volume named `credential-keys` has Secret != nil and *Secret.DefaultMode == int32(0o400)
and_then: this guards against the credential-keys directory in the fernet rotation Pod becoming world-readable

requirements.description: credentialRotationCronJob SHALL set FSGroup=42424 on the Pod template and DefaultMode=0o400 on the `credential-keys-src` Secret volume; the read-only `fernet-keys` Secret volume mounted by the credential rotate Pod SHALL also carry DefaultMode=0o400 (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: Symmetric to REQ-003 for the credential rotation Pod

scenarios.name: credential rotation Pod has FSGroup 42424
scenarios.when: credentialRotationCronJob is rendered
scenarios.then: the JobTemplate Pod template Spec.SecurityContext.FSGroup == 42424

scenarios.name: credential-keys-src carries defaultMode 0o400
scenarios.when: the rendered CronJob Volumes are inspected
scenarios.then: the Volume named `credential-keys-src` has Secret != nil and *Secret.DefaultMode == int32(0o400)

scenarios.name: fernet-keys (read-only mount) carries defaultMode 0o400
scenarios.when: the rendered CronJob Volumes are inspected
scenarios.then: the Volume named `fernet-keys` (the read-only Secret mount in credentialRotationCronJob) has Secret != nil and *Secret.DefaultMode == int32(0o400)

requirements.description: Both rotation CronJobs' `copy-keys` init container SHALL preserve a non-world-readable mode (0o400) on the EmptyDir working copy after the cp step (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: EmptyDir defaults to mode 0755 and `cp` from a 0o400 source preserves the source mode but creates new files owned by the init container's UID; the explicit `install -m 0400` (or `cp` + `chmod 0400`) step makes the post-rotation working copy auditable and avoids reliance on `cp`'s mode-preservation semantics across busybox/coreutils variants

scenarios.name: Fernet copy-keys argv contains an explicit chmod-equivalent
scenarios.when: the fernet rotation CronJob `copy-keys` init container Command is inspected
scenarios.then: the third element (the shell `-c` script) contains either `install -m 0400 /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/` OR `cp` + `chmod 0400 /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/*` (whichever the implementation chose)
and_then: the test asserts the substring `0400` is present so future refactors that drop the chmod step fail loudly

scenarios.name: Credential copy-keys argv contains an explicit chmod-equivalent
scenarios.when: the credential rotation CronJob `copy-keys` init container Command is inspected
scenarios.then: the script contains the analogous 0400 invocation against /credential-keys-src and /etc/keystone/credential-keys

scenarios.name: Init-container shell command remains POSIX `sh -c`
scenarios.when: the init container Command is inspected
scenarios.then: argv[0]=="sh" and argv[1]=="-c" and argv[2] is the script — the shape is unchanged so existing PSS Restricted assertions on the init container still pass

requirements.description: A chainsaw E2E test SHALL assert that the keystone container log emits zero matches for the substring `key_repository is world readable` against both the fernet and credential key paths within 30 s after pod-Ready (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: Negative log assertion is the only way to catch regressions in the kubelet projection chain (the rendered spec is necessary but not sufficient); the test also validates that the credential warning — which is currently masked by the fernet line firing first — does not appear

scenarios.name: Cold-start pod produces no fernet world-readable warning
scenarios.when: the chainsaw test waits for Ready=True and scrapes `kubectl logs <keystone-pod> -c keystone --tail=500` for 30 s
scenarios.then: no line matches the regex `key_repository is world readable: /etc/keystone/fernet-keys`

scenarios.name: Cold-start pod produces no credential world-readable warning
scenarios.when: the same log scrape is performed
scenarios.then: no line matches the regex `key_repository is world readable: /etc/keystone/credential-keys`
and_then: the test asserts both substrings independently — not a single combined regex — so a future regression on only one path is still caught

scenarios.name: Test fails clearly when the warning regresses
scenarios.when: the substring is found
scenarios.then: the chainsaw step exits non-zero with a message naming CC-0099 and printing the offending log line
and_then: this is the operator-facing acceptance signal

requirements.description: The Keystone reconciler reference doc SHALL document `fsGroup: 42424` on the Pod and `defaultMode: 0400` on every Fernet/credential key Secret volume (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: Doc-as-spec: the reviewer should be able to see the chosen mode without opening the Go source

scenarios.name: Deployment volume table updated
scenarios.when: the operator reads docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md
scenarios.then: the Deployment Volumes section lists `defaultMode: 0400` for both `fernet-keys` and `credential-keys`
and_then: the section also notes `fsGroup: 42424` on the Pod with a link to the upstream `_check_key_repository` rationale

scenarios.name: Fernet rotation CronJob volume table updated
scenarios.when: the operator reads the Fernet rotation section
scenarios.then: the table lists `defaultMode: 0400` for `fernet-keys-src` and the read-only `credential-keys` mount, and `fsGroup: 42424` on the Pod

scenarios.name: Credential rotation CronJob volume table updated
scenarios.when: the operator reads the credential rotation section
scenarios.then: the table lists `defaultMode: 0400` for `credential-keys-src` and the read-only `fernet-keys` mount, and `fsGroup: 42424` on the Pod

requirements.description: The change SHALL preserve the existing PSS Restricted contract: container-level SecurityContext returned by restrictedSecurityContext() remains unchanged for the keystone API container, the fernet-rotate container, the credential-rotate container, and both `copy-keys` init containers (CC-0099, CC-0045)
requirements.rationale: FSGroup is set at the Pod level; container-level fields (RunAsUser, RunAsGroup, ReadOnlyRootFilesystem, AllowPrivilegeEscalation, Capabilities.Drop=ALL, SeccompProfile) must remain bit-identical so CC-0045 PSS-Restricted assertions still pass

scenarios.name: API container SecurityContext unchanged
scenarios.when: the Deployment is rendered
scenarios.then: the keystone container SecurityContext equals the previous expectRestrictedSecurityContext() expectation

scenarios.name: Rotation containers' SecurityContext unchanged
scenarios.when: either CronJob is rendered
scenarios.then: the rotate container and the copy-keys init container both still satisfy expectRestrictedSecurityContext()

scenarios.name: Pod-level SecurityContext sets ONLY FSGroup
scenarios.when: either CronJob or the Deployment is rendered
scenarios.then: Pod.Spec.SecurityContext has FSGroup set and all other Pod-level fields nil
and_then: PSS Restricted does not require pod-level RunAsNonRoot when each container sets it; this scenario asserts we do not unnecessarily duplicate

requirements.description: Existing fernet and credential rotation chainsaw E2E tests SHOULD continue to pass without modification under the new file modes (CC-0099)
requirements.rationale: The chainsaw tests at tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/ and tests/e2e/keystone/credential-rotation/ already validate that rotation produces a new Secret hash without rollout and that a pre-rotation token validates after rotation; both must still hold under defaultMode 0400 + FSGroup 42424

scenarios.name: Fernet rotation chainsaw passes
scenarios.when: the test runs against a Keystone built from this branch
scenarios.then: the Job completes, the production Secret hash changes, pod UIDs are unchanged, the pre-rotation token still validates

scenarios.name: Credential rotation chainsaw passes
scenarios.when: the test runs
scenarios.then: the symmetric assertions hold for credential keys

scenarios.name: Manifest of existing chainsaw tests remains untouched
scenarios.when: the diff is reviewed
scenarios.then: tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml and tests/e2e/keystone/credential-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml are unchanged
and_then: the new world-readable assertion lives in a separate, focused chainsaw test (REQ-006) — not bolted onto the rotation tests, which would entangle two concerns

tasks.title: [RED] Add unit-test assertions in reconcile_deployment_test.go for Pod-level FSGroup=42424 and DefaultMode=0o400 on both `fernet-keys` and `credential-keys` Secret volumes (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-008, CC-0099). Tests must fail against the current implementation. WHAT: extend the existing `TestKeystoneController_BuildDeployment*`-style block at L270-315 with new sub-tests `TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_PodSecurityContextSetsFSGroup`, `TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_FernetAndCredentialVolumesSetDefaultMode0400`, `TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_ContainerSecurityContextUnchangedByCC0099`, `TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NoFSGroupChangePolicyOrUnsupportedFields`. WHERE: operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go. HOW: reuse the existing reconciler-runner helpers; reference openstackUID via the constant.

[GREEN] In reconcile_deployment.go buildKeystoneDeployment, add `Spec.Template.Spec.SecurityContext = &corev1.PodSecurityContext{FSGroup: ptr.To(openstackUID)}` and add `DefaultMode: ptr.To(int32(0o400))` to the Secret volume sources for `fernet-keys` (L246-253) and `credential-keys` (L254-261) (REQ-001, REQ-002, CC-0099). Reuse the openstackUID const from security_context.go — do not redeclare. WHAT: minimal edit; do not touch container-level SecurityContext or any other Pod-level field. WHERE: operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go (PodSpec at L166, Volumes at L246-261). VERIFY: tests from 1.1 pass.

[RED] Add unit-test assertions in reconcile_fernet_test.go for Pod-level FSGroup=42424 on the fernet rotation Pod template, DefaultMode=0o400 on `fernet-keys-src` and the read-only `credential-keys` Secret volume, init-container argv contains `0400` + `install`/`chmod`, and rotate-container/init-container SecurityContext bits unchanged (REQ-003, REQ-005, REQ-008, CC-0099). WHERE: operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet_test.go (extend the existing `TestFernetRotationCronJob_*` block around L600-697). New sub-tests: `TestFernetRotationCronJob_PodSecurityContextSetsFSGroup`, `TestFernetRotationCronJob_KeySecretVolumesSetDefaultMode0400`, `TestFernetRotationCronJob_CopyKeysInitContainerPreservesNonWorldReadableMode`, `TestFernetRotationCronJob_RotateContainerVolumeMountsUnchanged`, `TestFernetRotationCronJob_RotationContainerSecurityContextUnchanged`. Tests must fail against the current implementation.

[GREEN] In reconcile_fernet.go fernetRotationCronJob (L286-403), set `Spec.SecurityContext = &corev1.PodSecurityContext{FSGroup: ptr.To(openstackUID)}` on the Pod template, add `DefaultMode: ptr.To(int32(0o400))` to the `fernet-keys-src` SecretVolumeSource at L361 and to the read-only `credential-keys` SecretVolumeSource at L376, and change the init-container Command at L314 from `cp /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/` to `install -m 0400 /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/` (REQ-003, REQ-005, CC-0099). VERIFY: tests from 2.1 pass.

[RED] Add symmetric unit-test assertions in reconcile_credential_test.go for credentialRotationCronJob (REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-008, REQ-009, CC-0099). New sub-tests: `TestCredentialRotationCronJob_PodSecurityContextSetsFSGroup`, `TestCredentialRotationCronJob_KeySecretVolumesSetDefaultMode0400`, `TestCredentialRotationCronJob_CopyKeysInitContainerPreservesNonWorldReadableMode`, `TestCredentialRotationCronJob_RotateContainerVolumeMountsUnchanged`, `TestCredentialRotationCronJob_RotationContainerSecurityContextUnchanged`. Mirror the structure of the fernet tests added in 2.1; assert against `credential-keys-src`, the read-only `fernet-keys` mount, and `/credential-keys-src` paths.

[GREEN] In reconcile_credential.go credentialRotationCronJob (L289-407), set `Spec.SecurityContext = &corev1.PodSecurityContext{FSGroup: ptr.To(openstackUID)}` on the Pod template, add `DefaultMode: ptr.To(int32(0o400))` to the `credential-keys-src` SecretVolumeSource at L365 and to the read-only `fernet-keys` SecretVolumeSource at L380, and change the init-container Command at L317 from `cp /credential-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/credential-keys/` to `install -m 0400 /credential-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/credential-keys/` (REQ-004, REQ-005, CC-0099). implementation_notes:
Architecture: this is a one-field, three-call-site hardening. PodSecurityContext lives at PodSpec level (corev1.PodSpec.SecurityContext, type *corev1.PodSecurityContext) and is orthogonal to the existing container-level SecurityContext (corev1.Container.SecurityContext, type *corev1.SecurityContext) returned by restrictedSecurityContext(). They co-exist; the CC-0045 PSS Restricted contract is enforced at the container level and is not affected by setting Pod-level FSGroup.

Data flow (cold start): operator builds Deployment → kubelet schedules pod → projects fernet-keys Secret with DefaultMode=0o400 + FSGroup=42424 chgrp → effective directory 0o750, files 0o450 → keystone process (UID/GID 42424) reads files via the group bit → `_check_key_repository` no longer sees S_IROTH → no WARNING line.

Data flow (rotation): operator builds CronJob → kubelet schedules rotation pod with FSGroup=42424 → init container `install -m 0400 /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/` writes 0o400-mode files into the EmptyDir owned by openstack:openstack → rotation container runs `keystone-manage fernet_rotate` against the writable EmptyDir → script PATCHes the staging Secret → operator promotes onto production Secret on the watch event → kubelet re-projects production Secret onto the live API pod with new keys at 0o450, no rollout.

Key invariant: FSGroup and DefaultMode must land together. A partial fix that sets DefaultMode=0o400 without FSGroup would block keystone startup because the openstack process would lose group-read access (file would be 0o400 owned by root). The plan binds them in the same task pair (1.1/1.2, 2.1/2.2, 3.1/3.2) to prevent this.

Why `install -m 0400` over `cp -p && chmod`: single syscall path, fails atomically, no race window during which the EmptyDir is world-readable. BusyBox and coreutils both implement `install` with -m, and the keystone base image ships coreutils via images/python-base/Dockerfile (verified during research). If a future image swap drops `install`, the test asserts the substring `0400` AND either `install` or `chmod` so the fallback `cp + chmod 0400` form would still pass.

Why a new chainsaw test (key-repository-mode) and not bolting onto fernet-rotation: separation of concerns — the rotation tests verify rotation correctness; the new test verifies the absence of a specific log line. Mixing them entangles failure modes and makes diagnostics harder. The new test is also faster: no rotation step, just apply→Ready→grep.

Follow these existing files for the established style: reconcile_fernet.go:392 for `ptr.To(int32(0o555))` literal; reconcile_fernet_test.go:696-697 for the mode-assertion shape; reconcile_deployment_test.go:280-291 for the volume-by-name matching pattern.

ASCII flow:

```
  Keystone CR -> buildKeystoneDeployment -> Deployment(PodSpec.SecurityContext.FSGroup=42424,
                                                       Volumes[fernet-keys].Secret.DefaultMode=0o400,
                                                       Volumes[credential-keys].Secret.DefaultMode=0o400)
                                              |
                                              v
                                            kubelet projects:
                                              /etc/keystone/fernet-keys      drwxr-x---  root:42424
                                              /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/0   -r--r-----  root:42424
                                              (similar for credential-keys)
                                              |
                                              v
                                            keystone-manage runs as 42424:42424
                                              -> _check_key_repository: stat.S_IROTH not set
                                              -> no WARNING emitted   (REQ-006 satisfied)
```

Rotation flow:

```
  CronJob trigger -> Pod scheduled with FSGroup=42424
      InitContainer copy-keys (UID 42424):
          install -m 0400 /fernet-keys-src/* /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/
              -> EmptyDir files: -r-------- 42424:42424
      Container fernet-rotate (UID 42424):
          keystone-manage fernet_rotate against /etc/keystone/fernet-keys (writable)
          PATCH staging Secret with new key set + completed-at annotation
      Operator watch -> applyRotationOutput -> production Secret updated
      Kubelet re-projects production Secret onto API pod -> new keys live, no rollout
```

Deferred items: see deferred_items section.

Feature ID: every new test message, doc reference, and PR/commit reference must include CC-0099.
